title: Brandy tart – brandy isn't only meant for Coke

seo.title: Brandy tart - brandy isn't only meant for Coke - Marinda Kook

html:
In our house we regularly ate brandy/tipsy tart. Sometimes it was the dessert and sometimes the tart with coffee or tea. The versatile and easy tart can be baked in a large pie dish or as little tartlets in a muffin pan. It's delicious with ice cream, cream or custard. The tart freezes well. Make plenty and keep some for the day unexpected guests show up.

 

INGREDIENTS:

  • 200 g pitted dates
  • 5 ml baking soda
  • 250 ml boiling water
  • 125 ml butter or margarine
  • 250 ml castor sugar
  • 2 eggs
  • 500 ml cake flour
  • 5 ml baking powder
  • 2,5 ml salt
  • 5 ml ground cinnamon
  • 250 ml walnuts

SYRUP:

  • 310 ml sugar
  • 15 ml butter
  • 190 ml water
  • 5 ml vanilla extract
  • pinch of salt
  • 125 ml brandy

NOW:

  1. preheat the oven to 180 °C
  2. chop the dates finely
  3. pour boiling water and baking soda into a container and add half of the finely chopped dates to the water mixture – let cool
  4. cream the butter/margarine and sugar together
  5. add the eggs and beat further
  6. sift the dry ingredients together and add to the egg mixture
  7. chop the nuts finely
  8. now add the remaining dates and finely chopped nuts to the flour mixture
  9. lastly add the date-and-baking-soda mixture
  10. grease a pie dish and pour the mixture into your pie dish
  11. bake for 35 minutes at 180°C

SYRUP:

  1. boil the sugar, butter and water together for 5 minutes
  2. add the rest of the syrup ingredients
  3. pour the hot syrup over the hot tart

It sounds like a lot of work, but it isn't. For us as children it was grown-up pudding – probably because of the alcohol in it. Enjoy every bite – it's worth the effort.
